data.title: As a plugin maintainer, I can see commit workflow timing and success rates in the event log
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Instrument COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md to emit commit_workflow events at workflow start and end
**Why**: Commit workflow duration and failure rate are key bottleneck indicators — tracking them enables identification of slow or unreliable review stages
**Scope**:
- IN: Start event with wall-clock timestamp, end event with timestamp + success/failure + failure reason; incomplete commits detectable as unpaired start events
- OUT: Code review, plan review, fidelity review instrumentation

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md instructs agents to emit a commit_workflow start event before any timeout-prone steps and an end event with success/failure status
  <- Satisfies: 'Commit workflow events include wall-clock start/end timestamps'
- When this story is complete, incomplete commits (start without matching end) are detectable by the reporting script
  <- Satisfies: 'commit workflow failure rate calculations'
- When this story is complete, unit tests verify start/end event pairing and timestamp format
  <- Satisfies: unit test DoD

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Start event must be durably committed to the orphan branch BEFORE any timeout-prone steps (tests, lint, review dispatch) — SIGURG (exit 144) kills mid-workflow would otherwise lose both events. Emit and commit the start event first.
- [Reliability] Push bug 7895-e2a5 may prevent remote persistence
